title: Semiclassical Propagation and the Dynamics of Configuration Space
abstract: This work explores the non-relativistic quantum propagator $K(x,t)$ as a solution of the Schrödinger equation. We suppose that the propagator takes the form ${\rm exp}\left(\frac{\mathrm{i}}{\hbar}S+R\right)$, generalizing the usual WKB ansatz by allowing an additive exponent $R$ whose role as a measure of quantumness is investigated. Since $S$ is subject to the assumption that it is a solution of the Hamilton-Jacobi equation, here we are further interested in the role of $R$ and its interpretation as a measure of quantumness. Several systems are studied as concrete examples to illustrate this approach. Furthermore, a proposal to generalize it for fields is put forward. This is tested for some simple systems. Finally, the possibilities to use this approach for the case of systems whose dynamics are controlled by the Hamiltonian constraint are analyzed.
summary: This paper proposes a generalized semiclassical propagator that uses an additional real exponent to represent quantumness and entropy. It demonstrates that this formalism can unify quantum dynamics, field theory, and the thermodynamic interpretation of gravity in cosmological models.
main_problem: Investigating a generalized semiclassical ansatz for the quantum propagator where an additive exponent R is introduced to serve as a measure of quantumness and entropy.
main_result: The framework successfully recovers classical dynamics (Hamilton-Jacobi, Friedmann, and Klein-Gordon equations) in the limit h -> 0 and provides a link between the imaginary part of the action and gravitational entropy.
method: A semiclassical expansion of a generalized exponential ansatz K = exp(R + iS/h) applied to Schrödinger, Klein-Gordon, and Dirac equations, as well as minisuperspace models.
model_or_system: The study covers non-relativistic quantum mechanics (harmonic oscillators), quantum field theory (scalar and Dirac fields), and quantum cosmology (Robertson-Walker minisuperspace).
key_observables: The quantumness function R, the classical action S, and the functional momentum density.
important_parameters: The reduced Planck constant h, the scale factor a, the cosmological constant Lambda, and the semiclassical limit h -> 0.
main_assumptions: The classical action S is assumed to satisfy the Hamilton-Jacobi equation, and the expansion is truncated at the leading order in h.
paper_structure: The paper begins with non-relativistic particle models, extends the formalism to field theories (scalar and spinor), applies the ansatz to constrained cosmological systems, and concludes with a conceptual discussion on the thermodynamic connection to gravity.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
